title: Ledre Okins
content:
{{Top|fa|leg}} {{Character |type=Galactic Empire |image=[[File:Okins.jpg]] |name=Ledre Okins |homeworld= |birth= |death=[[5 ABY/Legends|5 ABY]], [[Colla system/Legends|Colla system]]''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]'' |species=[[Human/Legends|Human]]{{SWGTCG|set=The Shadow Syndicate|cardname=Admiral Okins}} |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Male]]''[[Shadows of the Empire Sourcebook]]'' |pronouns=He/him |height= |mass= |hair=[[Color/Legends|Brown]] |feathers= |eyes= |skin=Light |cyber= |families= |parents= |partners= |siblings= |children= |affiliation=[[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|I thought perhaps Admiral Okins might command the expedition.''"
"''Did you?''"
"''But if it is your wish, I shall lead the attack.''"
"''It is my wish. You may take Okins if you like, but you are to personally ensure the assault.|Darth Vader and Emperor Palpatine|[[Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire (novel)|''Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire'' novel]]}} '''Ledre Okins''', a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]], was one of the oldest [[Admiral/Legends|admirals]] in the upper echelons of the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. A by-the-[[Book/Legends|books]] [[Imperial officer/Legends|officer]], Okins obtained his [[Military rank/Legends|ranking]] through [[Standard year/Legends|years]] of strict loyalty to the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Empire]] and by simply following orders, rather than by taking great risks or being a tactical genius. Okins' loyalty came to the attention of [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]], who frequently dispatched Okins to lead task forces of [[Fleet Commander/Legends|fleet commanders]] who were not following Imperial regulations. Everything Okins observed he reported back to the [[Emperor/Legends|Emperor]]. In [[3 ABY/Legends|3.5 ABY]], shortly following the [[Battle of Hoth/Legends|Battle of Hoth]], the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] tasked Okins with overseeing the destruction of a secret [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] [[Vergesso Base|shipyard]] in the [[Vergesso Asteroids/Legends|Vergesso Asteroids]] from aboard Vader's personal [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]], the [[Executor-class Star Dreadnought/Legends|Super Star Destroyer]] [[Executor/Legends|''Executor'']]. Okins successfully led a [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]] that included the ''Executor'' and three supporting [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyers]] in a [[Battle of the Bajic Shipyards|tremendous rout]] of the Rebel [[Shipyard/Legends|shipyard]], destroying hundreds of [[Starship/Legends|vessels]] in a great victory for the Empire. Okins [[Death/Legends|died]] two years later, in [[5 ABY/Legends|5 ABY]], at the hands of [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] Admiral [[Willham Burke]], who was jointly spearheading the New Republic's drive into the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]] to retake the Imperial [[Capital/Legends|capital]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]], [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]], following the deaths of the Emperor and Vader at the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]] in [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]]. An extended campaign of raids and feints between the two admirals along the [[Perlemian Trade Route/Legends|Perlemian Trade Route]] culminated when Burke tricked Okins into dividing his fleet by sending key forces to the [[Pindra system/Legends|Pindra system]], allowing Burke to finally [[Battle of Colla|destroy]] Okins at [[Colla system/Legends|Colla]] in a major New Republic victory. ==Biography== ===The Emperor's toady=== [[File:Admiral-okins.jpg|thumb|right|170px|Admiral Ledre Okins in 3.5 ABY]] Ledre Okins was a highly-placed [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]] [[Admiral/Legends|admiral]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]].{{CSWECite|II|406|Okins, Admiral}} Okins toiled through [[Standard year/Legends|years]] of loyal service to the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Empire]] to reach his position, but his rise through the [[Military rank/Legends|ranks]] was not marked by powerful political connections, nor ambitious allies looking to use Okins to further their own standing. Rather, Okins established himself on merit alone, by demonstrating daily competence and simply following orders, and he owed neither favor nor loyalty to anyone but the Empire itself. As a result, Okins was older than many admirals in the Imperial Navy's higher echelons. Okins' unwavering loyalty to the Empire brought him to the attention of [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]], who considered Okins the perfect lackey to do his bidding. The [[Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] often assigned Okins to lead small task forces to eradicate [[Pirate/Legends|pirates]] or subjugate [[Planet/Legends|planets]]. Typically, Okins was given authority over a [[Fleet Commander/Legends|fleet commander]] who had skirted Imperial regulations and acted on ego and ambition, and an [[Imperial officer/Legends|officer]] finding himself serving under Okins knew that everything that happened would be reported back to the [[Emperor/Legends|Emperor]]. On occasion, Okins was even assigned to some of the Emperor's most trusted [[Advisor/Legends|advisors]], including the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]], to ensure his master's wishes were carried out. Shortly following the [[Battle of Hoth/Legends|Battle of Hoth]]{{WEGCite|story=Temporary Reassignment|book=Shadows of the Empire Sourcebook}} in [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]],The short story ''[[Temporary Reassignment]]'' from the ''[[Shadows of the Empire Sourcebook]]'' is set shortly following the events of [[Star Wars: Episode V The Empire Strikes Back|''Star Wars'': Episode V ''The Empire Strikes Back'']], which corresponds to the [[Standard year/Legends|year]] [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]], according to ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''. Vader returned to the Imperial [[Capital/Legends|capital]] planet of [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] aboard his [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]], the [[Executor-class Star Dreadnought/Legends|Super Star Destroyer]] [[Executor/Legends|''Executor'']], to make his report to the Emperor regarding his [[Duel on Cloud City/Legends|encounter]] with his son, [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]], on [[Bespin/Legends|Bespin's]] [[Cloud City/Legends|Cloud City]]. Vader brought Okins along with him so that [[Fleet Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Firmus Piett/Legends|Firmus Piett]], the ''Executor''{{'s}} [[Commanding officer/Legends|commanding officer]], could stay with the [[Death Squadron/Legends|Imperial Death Squadron]], Vader's [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]], to continue the Empire's hunt for the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]].''[[The New Essential Guide to Characters]]'' ===Vergesso Asteroids assault=== {{Quote|Have my Interceptor readied.''"
"''My lord, do you think that is—?''"
"''—wise? It has been too long since I flew in combat, Admiral. I need to flex those muscles. You can handle the shipyard.|Darth Vader and Admiral Okins|}} In 3.5 [[Galactic Standard Calendar|ABY]],''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' dates the events of the [[Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire (novel)|''Shadows of the Empire'' novel]] to [[3 ABY/Legends|3.5 ABY]]. Okins was present on Coruscant when the Empire learned of the presence of a flourishing [[Vergesso Base|Rebel shipyard]] in the [[Vergesso Asteroids/Legends|Vergesso Asteroids]], which had been operating under Imperial noses. The Emperor tasked Vader with the responsibility of personally overseeing the [[Shipyard/Legends|shipyard's]] destruction, against Vader's own preference. Vader did not consider it necessary that he go along for the assault, believing any half-competent line [[Officer/Legends|officer]] could see it through to successful completion, so he selected Okins to join him in command of the ''Executor'' for the expedition. Outside the [[Imperial Palace/Legends|Imperial Palace]] in Coruscant's [[Galactic City/Legends|Imperial City]], Vader ordered Okins to assemble a task force in preparation. Supporting the ''Executor'' would be the [[Imperial II-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial II''-class Star Destroyer]] [[Avenger/Legends|''Avenger'']] and two [[Victory II-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Victory II''-class Star Destroyers]], the ''[[Victory 1]]'' and the ''[[Victory 2]]''.''[[Star Wars: X-Wing Alliance]]'' [[File:Vader-Okins-SOTEE.png|thumb|left|260px|Admiral Okins and Darth Vader man the ''Executor''{{'s}} bridge during the Vergesso assault.]] Nearly a [[Standard week/Legends|standard week]] after earning the assignment, Okins and the ''Executor'' arrived in the [[Lybeya system/Legends|Lybeya system]] of the [[Bajic sector/Legends|Bajic sector]], home of the Vergesso Asteroids. The Rebel shipyard, a giant [[Vergesso Prime/Legends|asteroid]] housing hundreds of docked [[Starship/Legends|starships]] undergoing repair, provided less than a challenging operation. Nevertheless, Vader ordered Okins to open fire as soon as the fleet reached optimum distance, wanting no mistakes. Before Okins could bring his task force to engage the shipyard, however, several Rebel [[Capital ship/Legends|capital ships]] emerged from behind the [[Asteroid/Legends|asteroid]] to meet the Imperial attackers, and suddenly Okins was faced with a far greater challenge than simply eliminating a repair yard. The Rebel capital ships included two [[EF76 Nebulon-B escort frigate/Legends|EF76 Nebulon-B escort frigates]], vessels once belonging to the Empire but which had since fallen into Rebel hands. Okins remarked to an angered Vader that at least the stolen [[Frigate/Legends|frigates]], originally equipped to support Imperial [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighters]], would not be unleashing any [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE Fighters]] upon them. Nevertheless, on cue the Rebel frigates deployed a dozen [[T-65 X-wing starfighter/Legends|X-wing fighters]] to meet the Imperial task force, to the annoyance of Okins. He ordered the ''Executor''{{'s}} [[TIE operations officer]] to scramble their own fighters in response, not caring to waste the ''Executor''{{'s}} firepower on such insignificant targets. Still, the presence of Rebel fighters proved enticing to Vader, who chose to join the [[Battle of the Bajic Shipyards|battle]] in his own custom [[Darth Vader's TIE Advanced/Legends|TIE interceptor]], despite Okins' voiced reservations, leaving the destruction of the shipyard solely in the admiral's hands. Okins led the Imperial task force in eliminating the minor Rebel resistance and obliterating the shipyard, destroying hundreds of Rebel vessels and thousands of [[Troop/Legends|troops]] within—a great victory for the Empire. As soon as the assault concluded, the ''Executor'' immediately returned to Coruscant so that Vader could return to his domestic affairs. ===Destruction at Colla=== Okins' tenure as admiral continued into the period of chaos that followed the climactic [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]] in [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]], in which the Rebel Alliance struck a decisive blow against the Empire that saw the [[Death/Legends|deaths]] of both the Emperor and [[Darth/Legends|Darth]] Vader, and the splintering of the Imperial Navy into feuding factions. In the [[Standard month/Legends|months]] following [[Endor/Legends|Endor]], the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]]—the Rebellion's successor state—began its drive into the heart of the Empire to reclaim the [[The galaxy/Legends|galactic]] capital, Coruscant, opposed by a series of Imperial [[Warlord/Legends|warlords]] who had carved out independent territories from the fragmented Empire proper.''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' By the year [[5 ABY/Legends|5 ABY]], Okins, remaining loyal to the fragmented Empire, had established a base for his forces at [[Colla IV/Legends|Colla IV]], an Imperial [[Fortress world/Legends|fortress world]] in the [[Inner Rim/Legends|Inner Rim]] near the major [[Perlemian Trade Route/Legends|Perlemian Trade Route]]. The New Republic advance toward Coruscant soon pitted Okins and his forces in a campaign against elements of the [[New Republic Third Fleet|New Republic Third Battle Group]], commanded by Admiral [[Willham Burke]], who was tasked jointly with cutting off Imperial Warlord [[Treuten Teradoc]] from the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]]. Okins' forces met Burke's first in the neighboring [[Chazwa system/Legends|Chazwa system]], igniting a lengthy series of raids and feints between the two admirals that culminated ultimately in Okins' death that year. Okins met his defeat when Burke tricked him into diverting a vital part of his fleet to [[Pindra system/Legends|Pindra]], where it was ensnared in a [[grav trap]] that Burke had set among the [[Star system/Legends|system's]] [[Pindra system asteroids|asteroids]], allowing Burke to run down and [[Battle of Colla|destroy]] Okins at [[Colla system/Legends|Colla]]. Okins' defeat proved a major New Republic victory that opened the way for Burke to advance further Coreward down the Perlemian and link up with New Republic holdings at [[Brentaal IV/Legends|Brentaal IV]]. ==Personality and traits== {{Quote|Scramble our fighters. I don't want to waste firepower swatting these annoying…flies with our big guns.|Admiral Okins, during the Battle of the Bajic Shipyards|}} Ledre Okins embodied perfectly the Imperial Navy's by-the-manual officer. He climbed the Imperial Navy's command ladder not by taking great risks or with tactical genius, but by simply doing what he was told and following the orders of his superiors. Even though he was one of the oldest upper-echelon admirals in the Imperial Navy, the middle-aged Okins seemed older than he truly was due to his many weathered years of loyal service. The admiral's by-the-[[Book/Legends|book]] practicality demonstrated itself during the Imperial attack on the Rebel asteroid base in the Bajic [[Sector/Legends|sector]]. When faced with attacking Rebel starfighters, Okins elected to mobilize his own fighters for the sake of conserving firepower, despite having the overwhelming offensive advantage of the Super [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]] ''Executor'' at his command. Later, Okins voiced concern for Darth Vader's decision to personally enter the [[Starfighter combat/Legends|dogfight]] in his own fighter, believing the move to be tactically unwise, although he deferred to Vader's command with proper military respect, having no other recourse. Okins was bested tactically by Admiral Willham Burke during the New Republic's drive toward Coruscant, tricked into dividing his fleet, which ultimately led to his destruction at Colla. Okins never considered himself manipulated by the Emperor. He saw his work for the Emperor as merely his way of serving the Empire, because, in his mind, being a loyal servant meant doing whatever one was told, not pursuing personal agendas or trying to destroy one's peers. Should orders be ignored, the chain of command would collapse, and the Emperor's will would not be realized. Okins considered his link in that chain vital, no matter how minor. A [[Human/Legends|Human]], Okins had [[Color/Legends|brown]] [[Hair/Legends|hair]] and light skin. ==Skills and abilities== Okins possessed knowledge of planetary systems and capital ship and fleet tactics. He had mechanical ability in [[Astrogation/Legends|astrogation]], capital ship [[Pilot/Legends|piloting]], [[Comm unit/Legends|communications]], and [[Sensor/Legends|sensors]]; and technical proficiency in [[Computer/Legends|computer]] programming and repair, and security. ==Equipment== Admiral Okins donned the standard-issue [[Imperial officer's uniform/Legends|Imperial officer's uniform]] aboard the ''Executor'' during the assault on the Vergesso Asteroids.[[Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire Ertl minicomic|''Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire'' Ertl minicomic]] At other [[Time/Legends|times]] he omitted wearing the [[Imperial military uniforms/Legends|uniform's]] [[Hat/Legends|cover]]. His equipment included a [[Blaster pistol/Legends|blaster pistol]], [[Comlink/Legends|comlink]], and [[Datapad/Legends|datapad]]. ==Behind the scenes== Admiral Ledre Okins first appeared as a minor character in the [[May]] [[1996]] novel [[Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire (novel)|''Shadows of the Empire'']], written by [[Steve Perry]]. The [[June]] 1996 ''[[Shadows of the Empire Sourcebook]]'', written by [[Peter M. Schweighofer|Peter Schweighofer]], provided Okins with a brief background as one of the Emperor's lackeys in the Imperial Navy. Artist [[Ray Lederer]] provided the sourcebook's illustration of Okins, the character's first [[canon]]ical depiction. An unidentified mustachioed Imperial officer bearing a strong resemblance to Okins stands with Darth Vader on the ''Executor''{{'s}} [[Bridge (starship)/Legends|bridge]] during the Vergesso Asteroids assault in the [[Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire Ertl minicomic|''Shadows of the Empire'' Ertl minicomic]]. This article considers this character to be Okins based on the aforementioned corresponding details. The Okins character was later featured in the [[September]] [[2009]] ''[[The Shadow Syndicate]]'' expansion set of the ''[[Star Wars Galaxies Trading Card Game]]''. The set's "Admiral Okins" card presented the first confirmed color illustration of the character, artwork created by [[Kieran Yanner]]. The character's first name, "Ledre," was first created for the [[April]] [[2012]] sourcebook ''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]'', co-written by [[Jason Fry]] and [[Paul R. Urquhart]], which also established Okins' death in the years following the events of ''Shadows of the Empire''. ==Appearances== *[[Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire (novel)|''Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire'' novel]] {{1st|in book}} *[[Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire (abridged audiobook)|''Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire'' abridged audiobook]] {{Mo}} *[[Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire (junior novelization)|''Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire'' junior novelization]] {{Mo}} *[[Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire Ertl minicomic|''Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire'' Ertl minicomic]] ==Sources== {{Mediacat|imagecat=Images of Ledre Okins}} *''[[Shadows of the Empire Sourcebook]]'' {{1stp}} *{{Topps|set=1996 Topps Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire|cardname=Vader Destroys the Rebel Base|link=https://www.rebelscum.com/photo.asp?image=/sote/soteTC029.jpg}} *''[[Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' *''[[A Guide to the Star Wars Universe, Third Edition, Revised and Expanded]]'' *''[[The New Essential Guide to Characters]]'' *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' *{{SWGTCG|set=The Shadow Syndicate|cardname=Admiral Okins}} *''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]'' {{1stID|Ledre}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |nl=Okins }} {{DEFAULTSORT:Okins, Ledre}} [[Category:Humans]] [[Category:Imperial Navy admirals of the Galactic Empire]] [[Category:Individuals with he/him pronouns]] [[Category:Males]]
